name: Piedmont Housing Alliance
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor and affordable housing developer serving central Virginia with HUD-approved housing counseling, community management, and financial education.
description_long:
Piedmont Housing Alliance is a community-based non-profit organization operating in Planning District 10, covering Albemarle, Greene, Fluvanna, Louisa, and Nelson counties, plus the city of Charlottesville, Virginia. The organization was founded to address housing accessibility and community stability in the region.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of services centered on housing stability and financial empowerment. These include HUD-approved housing counseling, affordable housing development and management of 10 properties across their service area, certified resident services through the CORES program, a lending program certified by the US Treasury as community development financing, and financial education programs for hundreds of neighbors annually. They take a holistic approach to helping families and individuals navigate housing and financial challenges.

Piedmont Housing Alliance distinguishes itself through its integrated model combining property management with resident support services. Rather than simply providing housing units, they actively engage residents through programs for children and adults, financial literacy education, and community-building initiatives. Their CORES certification demonstrates rigorous commitment to resident services beyond basic housing provision. The organization also operates a lending program with formal US Treasury certification, indicating compliance with community development finance standards.

The organization is genuinely non-profit and free-help focused, making it appropriate for individuals and families unable to afford commercial credit counseling or housing consulting services. However, their service area is geographically limited to central Virginia's Planning District 10, and detailed information about specific counseling methodologies, counselor credentials, or lending program terms is not fully disclosed on their website. Prospective clients should contact them directly to understand service availability and specific program parameters.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling services at no cost through non-profit model
CORES certified for resident engagement, indicating rigorous quality standards for community support
Integrated services combining affordable housing, property management, and financial education under one organization
US Treasury-certified lending program providing community development financing as alternative to predatory lenders
Serves vulnerable populations including families with children through dedicated resident services programs
Operates actual affordable housing properties (10 managed units), not just advisory services
Covers six-county region with populations lacking local housing resources
cons: Service area limited to Planning District 10 in central Virginia; unavailable to consumers outside this region
Website lacks specific details about housing counselor credentials, counseling methodologies, or program outcomes
Lending program terms, interest rates, and eligibility criteria not disclosed on website
No information provided about average counseling session duration, availability, or wait times for services
Limited transparency about financial education curriculum or assessment metrics for program effectiveness
best_for: Low-income families and individuals in central Virginia seeking affordable housing with integrated support services
Renters and prospective homebuyers in Charlottesville and surrounding counties needing HUD-approved housing counseling
Residents of Piedmont Housing Alliance properties needing community support, financial literacy, and resident services
Small business owners and community members in Planning District 10 seeking Treasury-certified community development lending
diagnosis: Piedmont Housing Alliance is best for low-to-moderate-income individuals and families in central Virginia (Planning District 10) seeking free, non-profit housing counseling combined with affordable housing access. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—their services are only available in Albemarle, Greene, Fluvanna, Louisa, and Nelson counties plus Charlottesville; consumers outside this region cannot use their services.
services: Financial education programs for children and adults
Resident services and engagement through CORES certification
Programs addressing housing crisis and community stability
Resident support programs beyond basic housing provision
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Non-profit housing counselor serving central Virginia with HUD-approved counseling, community management, and financial education.
